review_text: We loved the grounds, the park was great.However, unfortunately our little one was very disappointed that there was no jumping pillow that we had promised her. It is her favourite part of camping. I would suggest updating your website as we probably would have gone elsewhere if we had known. Even the most recent reviews still spoke of the jumping pillow, so we had no idea until we were told on arrival. It probably sounds trivial to some, but it was very important to us.

Great place for a family holiday with plenty of accommodation options, from caravan sites to rooms and cabins. The park has a pool, playground, secure surroundings, and well maintained common areas. Check in and check out were also very easy and convenient. It’s a lovely spot to relax with family and kids. The only downside was the room insulation wasn’t very good, so you could hear people talking outside quite clearly. Overall still a very enjoyable stay.
Lovely Friendly Staff, and Beautiful  Park with Fabulous Cabins and Bedding !
What a beautiful spot to stop over night. Beautiful maintained, clean and tidy park. We have already decided to come back when we are next in the area. Simply beautiful morning walk around the lake.
Best Park we been to yet

Great clean holiday park. Excellent ablution block Fantastic camp kitchen Wonderful staff Sorry to drop down a star (would of been 1/2 a star if I could) The fire pits that can be hired aren't great when you didn't hire one but people near you did . We had to shut up early to avoid the smoke that came in our direction We didn't complain because people are allowed to do that but not good if you are down wind. I would return as it really is a great holiday park Thankyou
Great place

Wanted to give this park 5* & stay here frequently, but the radio playing in the bathroom is an instant turn off. What a terrible idea? Who wants to pay $$ to escape the city to be in nature, listen to the birds & water stream, take a deep exhale, only to have the news & commercials forced upon you to completely ruin the mood - stirring anxiety.

I hope this poor choice can be reconsidered, if you feel like music is a good choice maybe a nature loop soundtrack or relaxing spa music would be more appropriate & would have a better effect on the community.
We came for a rugby tour and spent a lot of time in the venue. The entertainment team, Rachel and Abi were great. They kept taking our song requests and made our evening

Amazing place to stay. Super clean, tidy and well maintained.  A safe place to that feels like they care. Looking at coming on an annual basis. The geodome was a unique glamping experience.  Thumbs up Big4

This establishment truly feels like a second home, a sanctuary of tranquility for us. Each visit surpasses the last, with the staff consistently providing exceptional service and the facilities exceeding all expectations. My hidden Gem 💎 ✨️

We stayed at the Yarra Valley holiday park for one night. If we had kniwn how nice the park was we would definately have stayed longer.|For families this is a greqt location. Lots of different play areas for the kids and a pool so the parents can join in. The site is in beautiful wooded setting and is very ckean and modern. The camp kitchens, there is more than one, are large well equiped and very nicely laid out. |The staff are so very polite and friendly and being big 4 members we got a late checkout and free coffee for visiting.|It was just my partner and I and we will definately be visiting again.

2 stars purely for the beautiful location.

We paid an astonishing amount of money to stay in one of the refurbished trams over a peak period as a treat for us and our train/tram loving child. It was very nicely refurbished. I'd recommend site 388 as it's private and the view from the deck is beautiful. The other tram is in a very high traffic area.

Unfortunately, that is where the good experience ended.

Both kids jump pads are broken and out of order - this is not mentioned on the website or on the maps we were given at check in. This was a big draw card for us so a huge let down. The "rec room" is a pool table and a bunch of paid arcade/skill tester machines, half of which do not work. You need to borrow pool cues and balls from the office however I only knew this after one of the other guests mentioned it to me - there is no signage. There is definately no fooze ball as shown on the website.

One pool is heated but is way too small for the size of the park. It was disgustingly murky and dirty by the end of the day.

Our booking came with a "complimentary: food basket which included two expired cheeses and wine that isn't even local (we're in the Yarra Valley?!).

When booking the tram you are told that the top bunk cannot be used by children older than 10yrs old due to weight restrictions, however the camp rules state children under 9yrs are not permitted on top bunks according to Australian standards and law...

The comforter on the main bed still had dried food on it from previous guests. The lounge had very visible stains. Kitchen was well equipped but had zero chopping boards.

Cherry on top was on our final night, finding mice nesting in one of the bed mattress bases. Could not wait to leave.

I understand this is not a luxury resort, I've stayed at caravan parks before, however for $800/night I expected much better.

I provided feedback on checkout and was assured the manager would call me to discuss. It's been 4 weeks and I have not heard back from anyone.

I was pleasantly surprised at how good the caravan park is - we were camping- the communal facilities where actually nice and very clean, camp kitchen is fabulous with numerous stinks and cooking spots so there's no waiting around to cook dinner or wash up, multiple big picnic tables so many families can seat together or separately (small tables for couples too), a huge TV screen (with the remote) so you can watch anything from toystory movie to afl, there's billards table tennis and and old school arcade game all in tge communal space in just one section of the park (there are numerous places like this scattered through out the park).
We stayed here as consultation holiday because fuel prices were so high we couldn't travel far but it was SO good we will eagerly come back just to holiday in the caravan park again.

Booked last minute with the Geo Dom, very happy, clean, cozy, great view, we booked late so no more Mega dom with bathroom, but it is not far either, a few meters away, nice and clean too, no smell. Very friendly staff. Kid love the fire pit near the creek, had amazing burnt Marsh.

We had a wonderful Easter at this holiday park. The park and facilities are well looked after and there was plenty for the kids to do. The stream running through the park was absolutely beautiful. This is one of the best holidays I have ever had.

Charged $100 for 2 bowls in the sink and a saucepan!. No photos. No mop in the van or - Management declared issues closed after emailing them.

Our group of 40+ people attended BIG4 Yarra Valley Park Lane for the second year in a row. We will not be returning.

The cleaning charge is absurd — I was told upon checkout that I had left two bowls in the sink. I immediately arranged for someone in our group who had not yet checked out to return to the van and clean them. They did. The $100 charge was applied regardless.

From that point, I requested photographic evidence of the alleged cleaning issue multiple times. Management refused to provide it. When I finally received a written response, the goalposts had shifted — the charge was now attributed to the condition of the bathroom and general cleanliness throughout the van. No photos were ever produced to support this.

I was travelling as a solo parent with three children under five over a weekend of continuous rain. There was no mop and no broom in the van. To charge a guest $100 when it rained most of the stay and then refuse to provide the photos that would justify it is not a fair or reasonable way to operate.

At $335 per night — nearly $800 for the stay — a standard guest turnover clean should be factored into the rate, as it is at every comparable property. Surcharges of this kind are reserved for damage or extraordinary mess. Neither applies here.

To make matters worse: the park's website advertised above-ground fire pits as available. It was a wet, cold weekend — exactly the conditions under which a fire would be welcomed and, critically, posed no risk whatsoever. We were told on arrival they were banned with no prior notice and no explanation that made sense given the conditions.

When I raised all of this with Operations Manager Michael Boyd, he unilaterally declared the matter 'closed'. When I indicated I would be leaving a review, the response was simply that they would have the opportunity to respond.

I'm sure they will. But the facts are straightforward: the original reason for the charge was rectified on the day. No photos were ever provided. The van had no cleaning equipment. And management chose to stonewall rather than resolve a $100 dispute with a returning group of 40 people.

We won't be back, and neither will our group.

Stayed this week.

Pros
* Great base to travel the Yarra Valley and surrounds.
* We stayed in a Superior 8 cabin we were upgraded from a 6 cabin. Great size cabin with lots of space. Kitchen and bathrooms were decent.
* Lots of play space for the kids to kick the ball and scoot around the park.
* Loved the creek at the back and the jump track hours of fun play.
* I thought at night it would be loud given all the camping but surprisingly it was quiet and nice.

Cons
* WiFi, nonexistent we were upgraded to an 8 ppl superior cabin at the back of the park which meant WiFi could not be reached and our 4G and 5G kept cutting out. If we wanted to use WiFi it was outside our cabin which is not ideal. Not sure l would consider this as an UPGRADE we didn’t need an 8 ppl room and then we lost WiFi. This was really disappointing and the front staff didn’t care. This also meant we were unable to use the TV with Netflix.
* Beds were so uncomfortable. Not sure what’s wrong with the bunks but my kids were over heating in them. Lucky we did have the extra double bed.
* 1 bed sheet was dirty.
* Lots of insects, the room should be sprayed before the next stay. I know you can’t help it but we had lots and they looked like they were around for sometime.
* Jumping pads- do not market these on your website if they aren’t available. I get one being broken but both! My kids were really disappointed.
* Swimming pools are very small considering the size of the park and the 14 ppl capacity is not manned.
* The games room is useless, need to upgrade the room and activities. Air hockey was out of order.

We liked this BIG4 for camping, but during the labour day long weekend there were too many guests. Despite temporary additional showers and toilets it was too busy. There are nice swimming pools but also very crowded that weekend. Park is well situated to go to places in neighbourhood. Bouncy pillow was broken.

This is a fabulous campsite, amazing facilities, as good as any we have ever experienced in the UK so it was a joy to spend a could of nights here. The manager was super friendly and amazingly helpful, above and beyond. Well worth a visit and stay if you are in this neck of the woods. We had a camper so only used the hook up and communal kitchen facilities.
Beautiful peaceful family holiday
It's one of the best caravan parks we've been to. I haven't seen a better camp kitchen and the amenity blocks are lovely and clean.

We stayed here over the weekend and while there were a couple of positives, overall it was a disappointing experience.

The small creek running behind our site was lovely, and I did appreciate being placed next to another family we were travelling with. That made things easier and more enjoyable.

Unfortunately, the facilities were a major letdown. The cleaning sign-off sheets in the closest bathrooms showed they were last cleaned on Thursday, and they were not cleaned at all before we checked out at 10am on Monday. By that point they were filthy, smelly, with bins overflowing with used paper towel and no toilet paper available. That’s simply not acceptable for a busy caravan park.

The advertising is also misleading. Current photos and the site map clearly show a jump pad, in fact two, but both have been removed with nothing in their place. This was really disappointing, especially when travelling with children.

Our powered tent site was also very uneven, with large bricks and rocks sticking out of the ground. We managed to set up right at the back on a small patch of grass, but it meant we had to go out and buy an extension cord just to access power.

As others have mentioned, the swimming pools are far too small for a park of this size. Even when we tried to swim on Sunday afternoon after things had quietened down, there were only around 10 to 12 people in the pool and it still felt overcrowded with very little room to actually enjoy it.

Overall, while the setting has potential, the lack of maintenance, cleanliness, and inaccurate advertising made this stay pretty disappointing.

We have been purchasing plants fron this company for longer than I can remember, and what a treat to venture into a labyrinth of colour. There was great entertainment from Circus Oz.  A good selection of meals and drinks. And plenty of gift shops to spend on your loved one, and myself 😉 overall we had a great time.

We had 3 powered sites for the weekend. The park is delightfully clean and well appointed. The Camp kitchen would have to be the most elaborate and well set up camp kitchen we have seen in nearly 30 years of travelling around Australia! The Staff were lovely and the grounds were well kept.

This caravan Park is amazing. Set amongst the trees, It has multiple on-site cabin options with something to suit everybody. Loads of little kids running around having an absolute ball. The little bike track is used 12 hours a day. The best thing however, was the showers. These are modern with an excellent flow of hot water. Might be better than what you've got at home.

Very disappointing and misleading experience.

Before making the booking, we specifically called the park to confirm whether we would be allowed to use a charcoal BBQ. We were clearly told over the phone that this would be permitted. Relying on that information, we went ahead and booked a caravan site.

Upon arrival, however, we were informed that no open fires are allowed at all. This directly contradicts the information we were given prior to booking and left us in a difficult position after already committing to the stay and bringing equipment specifically for that purpose.

What makes this even more frustrating is that the official websites of the Country Fire Authority (CFA) and Fire Rescue Victoria show that there are currently no fire bans in Victoria this weekend. After recent rains, the countryside is also very green and conditions are far from high risk.

If the park has a strict internal policy against charcoal BBQs, that is entirely its prerogative. However, providing incorrect information over the phone before taking a booking is unacceptable. Accurate information is the bare minimum customers should be able to rely on when making plans.

Based on this experience, it is difficult to trust the information provided by this park when planning a stay.

Tried a Glamping tent for the first time. The reception girls where very friendly and welcoming and got us in our dome early compasionatly due to being heavily fatigued from a long drive.

Soon as we got in we slept 2 hours later had 2 landscapers directly next to to tent reving the hell out there tractor and shaking the tent waking us up.

2 night stay nonone botherd to restock coffee and milk.  And some brainiac thought it would be a good idea to put some kinda table thing over the foot end of the mattress where ur feet vant even move under it constantly kickin it accidently

Also the free wifi amd tv in room is usless there is bearly a signal

Posative note: good matress  was good aircon is great ams nice view from inside

The area is absolutely stunning and I was happy in the geo done that I stayed in. It was comfortable, well equipped and clean. I did notice though that it is sitting on wooden slats with gaps that are open to the outside. I hate spiders so I was a bit unsettled. Bathroom facilities are small but clean and sufficient. There is a lovely bbq area with excellent equipment to use for free.
The only draw back is the noise levels. If you are not here for a family holiday it can be tedious with constant yelling and screaming kids until around 11pm at night. Kids running everywhere unsupervised and adults yapping loudly non stop. Made our stay very unpleasant at times. Unfortunate the family next to us in the mega dome were yapping loudly on their verandah all day.
The pool is very small and impossible to swim in with kids jumping and screaming unsupervised while adults enjoy some time out. This was just after school holidays period.

I love this park. Its so conveniently located from Melbourne for a quick weekend away. There is something for everyone. We've stayed in the older cabins (yes, probably time for an update here), safari tent/cabin (amazing!), unpowered site by the creek and ensuite site: they are all great. The camp kitchens are excellent, there's a pool, jumping pillow, play grounds, pump track and nature walks; all set in beautiful natural surrounds of the lush rolling hills, close to town and a plethora of activities. But the best thing about this place is the people who run it - they are simply awesome. I've never had a negative experience. They are helpful and friendly and while you wait for you great coffee they are full of tips on whats happening around the place. Can not recommend more!!!

Had a caravan site on lower level, close to amenities and camp kitchen because the weather was turning ugly. Wow...great choice. An absolutely amazing space to spend hours in, very comfortably! Everything had been thought of and a great range of cooking/dining equipment provided. Only tiny negative point I would say was they probably should have had more loos/showers in that block...it would be a wait if the park was busy. They were however very clean and modern.
Great family experience, close to town so you aren't too far in the sticks, and the amenities are great with 2 pools, 2 games areas and a kiosk. BBQ on site also.

I stayed in a Glamping Pod.
It was the absolute best!
Clean, comfortable and quiet.
The staff are friendly and accommodating and the facilities are top quality 👌

We stayed in a Historic Melbourne WClass tram. It was amazing. So comfortable, 3 full bathrooms and full size lounge in the added Tram Stop house. We loved the efforts to keep the tram authentic but updated. The Park is so big with so many different types of Accommodation. Would highly recommend fir any age group.
We stayed in the tram over Christmas and had the most wonderful stay. Love the location and the creek and the kids loved the skate track. Will definitely stay again

If I could give more stars I would. The whole staff team yesterday and today have been amazing, nothing has been too much trouble for them.
If you are looking to stay in the Healsville area definitely book this site, you will not regret it, not only are the staff amazing the site is also great too.
We stayed in the Glamping safari tent amazing!

A inconsiderate camper had parked in our booked spot and the big4 management could not be bothered to come down and dislodge the human tick so we was forced to moved to a site that was not an actual camping spot and we could bearly reverse our caravan into place.
I will never return to the caravan park again.

Overall a good park with lots to do for kids. The cabin we stayed in had a full bin of rubbish upon check in and had cob webbs on the light fittings. The jumping pads were also not working correctly, look old and would be good to have the jumping pillows instead. The pump track also needs to be realligned as some gaps are opening up and could result in injuries. On a busy warm weekend the pool size is very squeezy aswell. The older bbq kitchen also very hot and not pleasent to eat in.
A great place to stay
Great park facilities location staff we couldn't have asked for more,close to Healesville in the middle of the Yarra valley.Brilliant

I loved the stay. Wish I had stayed for longer. The site was very tranquil and I enjoyed sitting by the creek alot. Only thing I found to be an issue was, the cleanliness of my room. It wasn't horrible. But could have been better.
Lots of different accommodation to choose from!! Rubbish piling up isn't a great look!

We stayed on a powered and water site in our caravan.   We stayed in a group and to be able to have sites together we had to pay and extra $25 per site to guarantee this.  We thought it was a bit uncalled for, especially when we booked we were able to be put together then upon confirming booking a week out they told us we had to pay.
Park is close to Healesville and activities but not walking distance.  Plenty of different accommodations to choose from.
Pool was too small for the park size, 1 shipping container pool which was quite warm and 1 smaller pool with an area for littlies, but on a 40deg day there was no room to move.  Both pools were very cloudy and the bottom pool you could not see the bottom let along your knees in the water.
There's a creek running along the caravan park which was delightful to sit in and chill, no good for very little kids or one's that arnt confident on rocks because its very rocky. Good playgrounds, tennis courts,  bike track, really good for kids.

The best caravan park we have been to... several times over! This park has everything you need in a great location. Multiple activities for the kids, a great pool and an even better clear river with Multiple shallow spots perfect for kids of all ages.
Caravan spots of all sizes, and plenty of space around you so even when its busy, you don't feel crowded.

DO NOT BOOK TOO EARLY , WILL COST SUBSTANIALLY MORE $$$$$
Well and truly overpriced $$$$.   We are Big4 members and booked directly on their website.
This is the most expensive 2 bedroom family cabin in Australia we have ever  stayed at. We travel regularly with our 2 grandchildren and always book 2 bedroom cabins in caravan parks for about 3 nights.
We have stayed in the following parks recently -  Turtle Beach resort Gold Coast, Discovery Park Echuca, Apollo Bay Holiday Park, Cobram Baroga Resort Park , Lorne Caravan park and their accommodation costs were very reasonable compared the RUTHLESS prices by th management and owners at the BIG4 Yarra Valley Park Lane Holiday Park  Healesville.
We stay for 3 nights in mid January 2026  ( Tues, Wed  & Thurs ) and because we are Big4 members, we received a MASSIVE $50  reduction,  SO  the 3 nights for 2 adults & 2 children was ONLY $1537. THATS $512 per night for a simple 2 bedroom cabin ( 30 m2 ). How can a Big 4 Park charge these exorbitant prices per night $$$$$$$$
Four days prior to our accommodation I checked their website and the same cabin type was just over $1000. Unfortunately they do not allow you to cancel and receive a refund. If this was the case, we would have re-booked and saved OVER$500.
We can now understand why the park had many vacant cabins in the middle of the School Holidays. The Park was not at all busy , which we assume is due to their ridiculous prices.
NOTE - Not long after we arrived, we discovered that the hot water system was faulty, some lights not working and the cabin had NOT been cleaned properly. Old food and sandwich garbage as well as other rubbish under all the couch cushions, very DISGUSTNG. We went to the Park reception and informed them of our issues. They immediately informed us that we can change to another cabin  and  we agreed if we could inspect the cabin first. The next cabin was an improvement.
As mentioned previously, another cabin was not a problem with all the current vacancies they we experiencing.
I would NOT RECOMMEND  this Park in anyway, even our very young Grandchildren thought it was very ordinary. They mentioned to us that we have stayed in a lot better Parks and cabins before. Small pools , 5 klms out of Healesville ,etc.
Only positive is that is close to the Healesville Sanctuary
We have just booked 8 nights in a  2 bedroom Superior Spa Villa ( 120 m2 ) at the Big 4 Ingenia Holidays Cairns Coconut Resort for $198 per night. Amazing price difference and cabin size from the Healesville, Victoria Big 4.
If you decide to stay here , ensure you book very close to the dates you wish and save heaps of money.
Ripped OFF and not happy

We booked the campsite, and being part of a group we were separated from the rest that were in Cabins.. unfortunately for us even though we booked months in advance and had kids with us weren't given the consideration of being in shade

Great caravan park, has two pool areas, lots of spots for kids to play, bike tracks, beautiful wildlife, the room we had was good a few little things would have helped make it more comfortable but I would stay here again.
Nice for the kids and the family. Especially of you are near the river. Wish the recreation area got cleaned and dusted.

Fabulous park, so many things for families to do. Amenities clean and tidy, camp kitchen has fridges, microwave, kettle, dishcloths etc. all that make your stay much easier. TV and entertainment for children, two pools, jumping castle type of thing, playground, pump track plus bar-b-q’s.

After being evacuated from Alexandra,
The management and reception staff were amazing, offering us a cabin to sleep 6 and a dog.
Having other locals around to hug and check on was also special.
Amenities were amazing, clean the park itself had everything we could hope for
BUT most of all compassion!
We thank you from the bottom of our hearts.

Stayed in a pod with shared bathrooms.$150 first night,$100 second night for 2 people. 4 friends stayed in a cabin with their own bathroom for $100 both nights.Hard to understand the pricing structure.

Most importantly, we were able to book a pitch here when all other sites nearby were full. The campground is in two distinct sections, the older, more natural area and the very modern, newer sections. We were in the newer section where the toilet/shower block was amongst the very best we have encountered in Australia and where our campervan pitch was spacious, flat and powered.
The reception staff were friendly and helpful and we would not hesitate to stay here again if in the area.
Fabulous! Best camp we ve ever been to .

Fabulous Park for us while evacuating the fires in Yea!
Very accomadating with our two dogs
Amenities very clean

This Park was more than accommodating of our needs.
We had been evacuated from Eildon the night before, I booked online at 7:30am and was in the park settled by 9am.
The staff were capable of answering any of our questions, kind and friendly.
Highly recommend this park

Absolutely Disappointing – Poor Facilities, Worse Management

This caravan park was a massive letdown and a complete waste of money. It is clearly overbooked and does not have the facilities to handle the number of guests they cram in.

Both pools were so overcrowded there was no room to even stand upright, let alone swim. The idea of enjoying the water was laughable. The games room was unusable — no fans, unbearable heat, and completely unsafe and uncomfortable for kids. Calling this “family-friendly” is extremely misleading.

The biggest issue, however, was management. After raising a formal complaint, Michael, the Operations Manager, dismissed our concerns entirely and said he “did not see a problem.” That response alone says everything about how this park is run.

We chose to leave after just one night due to how bad the experience was. When we requested a refund and clearly explained our reasons, we were offered a credit instead — for nights we didn’t even stay — despite making it clear we would never return. When I asked for the refund to be processed back to the original account (which they had already been paid in full from), I was bluntly told NO, with no reasonable explanation.

To make matters worse, when I asked for a supervisor’s contact details, Michael simply ignored the request and told me the matter was “closed.” Completely unprofessional and unacceptable.

This park is all about squeezing as much money as possible out of guests, with no care for comfort, safety, or customer service. It’s incredibly disappointing because it could be a great place — but bad management has ruined it.

Save your time, save your money, and do not bring your family here. We left stressed, frustrated, and deeply disappointed.
Still waiting at the reception room for checking in as the room is still unavailable. We requested to delay checking out tomorrow but they informed me that it's fully booked tomorrow.

This is our go to place for camping. It has lots of accommodation options (cabins, pods, campsites) as well as activities including inflatable jumping areas, kids playgrounds, lake with fishing options, tennis and basketball courts, swimming pool.
Location is great within close proximity of Healsville town.

Ring Ring Ring and book your stay, we made the mistake of not calling and asking for a powered site on the river and boy did we pay for it! We were on rocks with our camper, no concrete slab no shade, so close to our neighbours we could smell their food on all sides of us,  most of the people there couldn't even park next to your camper we had to park on the road. The noise from the community area was shocking, until 12pm every night even though there was a curfew. We didn't even have enough room on our plot to sit outside our awning so we were stuck inside on rocks and dirt in the heat. The 2 pools were overflowing with people so we couldn't have a swim either to cool down. So noisy and not a nice experience The place is beautiful surrounds but our plot was the worst, and as we were leaving they were stuffing more sardines in. We felt that they were just taking bookings just for the money and not taking into account the experience of what could have been so much better.

Schöner Campingplatz mit diversen Optionen vom Camping bis zu Hütten. Freundliches Personal, gute und saubere Waschküchen, Toiletten und Abwaschmöglichkeiten. Die Campsite selbst war ebenfalls sauber. Bei den Campsites ohne Strom gibt es keine zugewiesenen Plätze, es war jedoch recht wenig Andrang somit konnte man sich mit genügend Abstand platzieren.
Great facilities. Very well maintained.
Great family get away, very well maintained grounds & plenty of activities to keep the kids occupied.
When caravanning be sure to ask for a location AWAY from the Recreational room.  Noise and rubbish from this area was hard to put up with.
Amazing Big 4 and great service. I've been to a lot of big 4 parks but this one would be at the top of my list.
great rooms with lots of family friendly activities and great staff !

Still staying at the Superior Family Cabin that sleeps 8 people. So far nothing seems superior about it. First thing when we walked in for the dirty, moldy floors. At the end of the day your socks are so dirty! Floor seems to have some black mould or something which I don’t want to touch. The cabin air quality seems to be very dusty or have a lot of pollen as I cough non stop everytime we’re inside. Air quality seems to be cleaner and better outside than inside.

As stated the Cabin is suited for 8 people. They have heaps of knives and forks but MINIMAL plates and bowls, maybe around 3 plates, bowls and a few wide plates. Like seriously?!

Towels and face towels all have stains, some with some black marks and some dirt on it which we end up not using. WiFi is non existent here, can find it but cannot connect to it. Can only connect when you’re outside.

Coffee at the kiosk tastes horrible. Something that a kid would make, you have a fancy machine and grinder and yet you don’t know how to make a coffee.

Rec room is bare, air hockey has no puck after paying to play it. Pool table but no balls and sticks. Would thought they would have some activities for the kids to play with which we DON’T need to pay for since it’s a family park!

Management here is appalling, their first impression they seemed confused, I even gave them the cabin number we are staying at. And still got my name wrong after stating it the first time.

First time staying at this resort and would be the last.

Very clean toilet. Superb facilities.

Campground slightly uneven. Motorhome do take note.

Fantastic Park.
Friendly ,helpful staff. Great facilities .
Beautiful to walk around with lovely views.
My only concern was how fast some cars drive through the park, Wow Wee !!!
The park has several signs stating the speed limit and common sense should be used also.
PEOPLE SLOW DOWN !!!
We thoroughly enjoyed our overnight stay.  The pod is small but contains a king size bed and the essentials.  Beautiful setting amongst forest and streams.  Loved it.

Extremely poor customer service

After staying at BIG4 Yarra Valley Park Lane for three consecutive years on the same sites, we were very disappointed with how our loyalty was treated this year. Despite being long-term returning guests, the sites were booked to another group without any prior notice or opportunity to rebook before our stay was over - something that is standard practice at most parks.

This came on top of our previous disappointment with the lack of assistance when another campsite took our power. It feels like our loyalty was completely ignored, and sadly, we will not be returning.
Nice park. Great location. Don’t book directed with the park, go through online platforms. Prices are very dynamic and you’ll actually be charged more.

It was a really good experience. I didn't know about this place. It's about 1.5 hours from the CBD.

If you need a place for families or just couples, it's a good option. There are also some places nearby that you can visit.

Good service and it is a peaceful place.
Awesome surroundings loads of different accommodation options. The pods are cool and cheap.

The team at Park Lane were very helpful-  our tent sprung a leak (or 10) and the office was super helpful in helping to very kindly sort us out for so much dryer accommodation during the school holidays. Many thanks!!

Clean, and stocked with all you need in Rec and kitchen for cooking and eating. Crockery and utensils provided. Fridges and bbqs, also a microwave . Shared bathrooms supplied with personal items like body wash, shampoo etc and toilet paper.
Lovely park with different types of accomadation.

Had booked one of the Geo Domes for one night.
Unfortunately the intense chemical odour from the VOCs emitted by the freshly varnished/treated timber made remaining inside the tent for more than a few minutes unbearable.
We expressed our concerns to the front desk but were told all huts, cabins or sites were either booked out or unclean.
This left us with no other option than to leave and find alternative accommodation elsewhere.
When we emailed our concerns to management we were told a credit has been issued but due to no other complaints being made since the domes were erected 3 months ago a refund was not possible. How other guests managed to stay the night is astonishing.
For the health and safety of future guests I hope the Big 4 Yarra Valley consider investigating this further.

There was also exposed ant rid on the floor not ideal for those with young children.

Beautiful park. Have been very impressed with the park lane parks in general. Well set up sites for caravans and families. The ensuite was lovely. Lots of ammenities. Very well priced. Beautiful scenery around the park. Lots of space between sites. Staff were lovely

Definitely unpleasant experience!!!
We are here just for an one-night get away with my 6 month bub and 4yo. We were all excited about living in a Safari tent but we ended up laying next to a roaming digger!! And this ongoing construction is not notice anywhere!!!!! All the responses I have received so far from the park is just a "sory, they have to work"
But excuse me, is my $500 tent experience supposed to be like this? My bub cannot sleep at all.
Everything is perfect.
Great little glamping pod... could have used a couple of extra power outlets.

Book and paid for a 2 bedroom cabin.  Price was ridiculous due to a long weekend. Turned up to be lied to and told my friends had swapped my room.  Turns out they had put friends who also booked a 2 bedroom into a studio room .  Told us the rooms were the same price, no difference.  The rooms are completely different.

Also didn’t clean the rooms when moving people between them , just changed the sheets .  Bathroom not cleaned and no toilet paper
Absolute rubbish accommodation, incorrect advertising. Love sleeping in the kitchen 😩

I can not recommend this place enough. Having stayed last week at another Big4 that was questionable, this place is a luxury resort.

The bathrooms have heated flooring and absolutely beautiful showers. The sites are wide allowing for ample room to park and use as needed.

The laundry didn’t need coins! It’s just a tap on a phone or a card which is so much easier.

The amenities on site are above and beyond expectation with bouncing pillows, kiosk that serves coffee, great playgrounds and food trucks on site for the school holidays.

We are so much happier
Stayed in the Mega Dome - great experience. Great for a weekend getaway couple and kids or just a couple. Wake up listening to the rain on the dome

We’ve stayed at many caravan parks and this was our favourite by far! The whole park was spotless and beautifully maintained, and the location is just gorgeous. The kids had a fantastic time with plenty to keep them entertained, which made it really relaxing for us too. Having our own ensuite was a huge bonus – it was new, clean, and so convenient. A perfect family getaway, we can’t wait to come back!

I have been to BIG4 Yarra Valley Park Lane three times already, and each time has been a great experience. I tried three different stays – the glamping pod, the geo dome and the ensuit site, all of them were very clean and comfortable.

The park is well equipped with entertainment facilities and clean kitchen utilities, which makes it very convenient for camping beginners like us. The check-in and check-out process is always quick and easy.

A lovely place to relax and enjoy the Yarra Valley with family and friends. Highly recommended!
Lovley
What a great place.  Well very clean.  Thank you.  We will be back

We were in a caravan but had an ensuite which was five star luxury complete with beautiful toiletries and fresh towels.
I was disappointed that the rubbish bins were hard to find and too far from where we were (especially compared to other parks).

I stayed there with small children and one of the mattress was wet apparently as per the manager, they should have mattress protector as it was in the kids bed. They charged me $150 to clean which is ridiculous amount given the mattress itself isn’t worth $60 and when I tried to reason with them they were rude and didn’t even provide me with the invoice if the cleaning that they supposedly did and I would never go back there
My favourite park holiday
Very quiet and great amenities

as with all big4 sites, do expect good well maintained facilities including clean toilets (and this one here is heated as well), good well maintained astro tuff, friendly staff and professional procedures for check in. highly recommended.

Definitely a place to return for another holiday. We went with our two young boys (2 and 4) and extended family and it was an absolute blast! Very close to the Healesville Sanctuary and plenty for the kids to do throughout the park. Jumping pads, Rec room, playgrounds, pool, lake....so much to do! There's also a kiosk open daily if you would like a coffee and some hot food as well as bird seed available to feed the birds. Beautiful views and so quiet. We were lucky enough to enjoy an upgrade to a safari tent which was amazing!

I stayed in a glamping pod & it was great. Cosy & comfy. Staff were friendly.

I have one major complaint however - you can’t advertise a location as having wifi if it doesnt work. Staff seem to take the approach that its ‘just this part of the world’, but that isnt true. They need to invest in their infrastructure. Expand the network. Night time was incredibly frustrating as the mobile phone reception is also terrible. Internet is a very basic requirement these days.
We loved our stay at this holiday house! It was exceptionally clean and well maintained, making our visit comfortable.and beautiful location to be in. Really recommend

Fantastic place to bring the caravan with the family! This place really has everything and is very well maintained, amazing amenities and really helpful staff. We have been twice now and will absolutely come again

Favourite caravan park location by far. We will be back.

Swimming lake, amazing views of Yarra Ranges, creek running through the park and fantastic amenities for kids. From Adelaide, this was truly one of the best caravan parks we have visited and plan on coming back in near future.
Awesome camp site. Lots to see and do.
👏 place and nice staff.love the park so peaceful and quiet

Fantastic park!!
Under floor heating in the amenities block was a welcome treat.

This was our first experience staying at a holiday park with our family of 7 and we could not have been happier with the experience.
We stayed in the new Safari Tent accomodation. The room itself was  gorgeous, luxurious and spacious- even for our big family. It had everything we needed. We had a private decking overlooking the gumtrees with bbq and dining space where we watched the kookaburras each morning.
The park itself was stunning. The kids had so much space to run and play as well as multiple playgrounds.The highlight for the kids was being given some huge bags of bird seed at reception to feed the many friendly ducks on the lake. We couldn’t imagine a more kid friendly place. I don’t think we will ever stay in Airbnb or hotels again. Thank you for having us. We will definitely be back!!
Loved this place,  awesome for the kids, great facilities
We are staying here for 3 nights on a van site and highly recommend this fantastic park. The amenities are the best we've ever seen!

It's ok and we had fun but definitely do some research on dates before booking. Booked first week of May, 1 week after long weekends and they still claimed it was peak period and charged accordingly. Park was empty.... definitely would pay peak rates again for what you get.

The BIG4 in Healesville is absolutely fantastic. It has everything you need to keep the whole family happy, with well-thought-out facilities and activities for all ages. The staff and owners are friendly, attentive, and genuinely committed to outstanding customer service. Whether you’re planning a relaxing weekend escape or a fun family getaway, I highly recommend this place for a memorable country retreat.
Great caravan park in a beautiful area along side a little creek. Many caravan sites and cabins. Friendly staff and loads of bird life.
Very friendly staff, only downside is a few ks out of town.

We booked a single tent for our 1-year anniversary, drawn in by the photos online that showed an open-roof setup and a scenic, nature-filled vibe. When we arrived, we were extremely disappointed to find a fully closed tent located in a car park, with construction work as our view. The photos online were very misleading and did not reflect the actual experience. This was meant to be a special occasion, and it was a letdown.

My partner and I went there to celebrate our first anniversary. I had booked a tent that was supposed to be surrounded by nature and trees, giving off a camping vibe. However, we ended up in the middle of a car park, under the sun, on a concrete surface with no greenery—definitely not the fresh, natural setting we were expecting.

So, if you're planning to surprise your family, just be aware of what you're really paying $300 for.

Please see the photos. Thank you.

We had a great Easter stay at BIG4 Caravan Park! The kids loved the special holiday activities, and the staff were friendly and accommodating. While there were a few minor areas for improvement, overall, it was a fantastic family getaway. We'd definitely consider returning!​
Nice place for camping and good family and kids

The staff are wonderful, so friendly and helpful - fantastic customer service, the best I’ve received from anywhere in a long time. Park and facilities are stunning, very clean and so much available. I cannot wait to come back
Very nice facilities and located in the beautiful Yarra Valley. Worth visiting.
Nice caravan park, heated pool. Kids loved riding their bikes and the jumping balloons. Modern and clean camp bathrooms and kitchen. Good rec room although some items not working
Cant say about the rooms as we camped in our tent. The amenities were superb and we did check a few rooms for future events and they looked great

Is a lovely park.  Very clean.  Disappointing that there is a person flying a drone in the park.  It's annoying rude and inconsiderate.  Illegal.  Maybe too?

Oh and what a joke a bloke turns up in a range rover with a dog. And they say no fires yet this mob has fires as well.
The park started off so well yet has dropped its bundle badly.

Dear owner,

Thanks for your explanation one interesting comment you have made regarding the dog.  Your park doesn't accept dogs. So not sure how that fits your model..

Over technology with your children? Want a family adventure, no better place than the big 4 yarra Valley Park lane. They have it all...
Any type of accommodation they can arrange. From log cabins over looking a lake , or modern cabins that can sleep 8, with a clean creek flowing around. Not to mention tranquillity with the surprises of wildlife. Kookaburras, possum,  ducks with ducklings,  catch and realise fish. Playgrounds , heated pool + many other amazing things to do.
Highly recommended 👌
Thank you to the staff for making my husbands 31 birthday a memorable one.
Awesome caravan park, very well appointed, cabins looked good, we stayed in a powered site by the creek.

I stayed in this awesome caravan park with family 2 times and it never disappointed.. always in tent, I will surely try one of beautiful Belle tents soon - we had a quick sneak peek at them and they look so comfortable. The common areas are very clean, modern and we literally could arrive without any food items like dishes, tea towels, dish detergent and similar because it's all provided by the facility. My children absolutely loved the lagoon style swimming pool and heated swimming pool. Also the jumping pillows. Staff at the reception always very friendly too. Not forget to mention the fantastic location. Truly my favourite caravan park so far..
Gramping really nice but had shower mould.

Loved the big 4 Yarra valley.  We were on a caravan site.  The ladies in reception were lovely and welcoming.  Explained everything.  The park is big and spacious, grounds and amenities were spotless.  Especially liked the fact that supplied in the showers were shampoo/conditioner/body wash.  A lovely thought.  Nice pool area, playground.  Beautiful birds you can hand feed(seed provided to you by the park).  We thoroughly enjoyed our stay and will stay again when near the Yarra valley.  Only thing I suggest, is don’t get a site under trees.  We had gale force winds (one day, no fault of the park) and had a number of large branches drop on our van.  But there are plenty of sites that are not fully under trees.  There was a problem with the generators one day (once again no fault of the park.  Was a hot hot day and obviously everyone had their coolers on) but this was rectified by the park. They provided late checkout for everyone to compensate, which we thought was a lovely gesture.  So we can highly recommend this lovely, well kept park.
Great caravan park with exceptional facilities, modern clean and well maintained. Perfect location for exploring the yarra ranges

Camper van site with en-suite for a couple of nights. Good location, quiet, nice coffee shop/ cafe, small pool and jumping pillow.
We were allocated one of the older en-suites which were a bit drab and very unlike the modern ones shown on the website. Overall a nice weekend away.

The park itself was great. The kids had a ball. The park need to install boom gates ASAP as we had 4 cars broken into and one car stolen from the park and the second night at midnight 2 more cars came through the park, did laps then left. We realised our friends car was stolen and our cars ransacked and wallets stolen at 7am. Went to the office to get help but did not get anyone from the park come to see us until 10:30am. We had police with us for over an hour in their park who found other park guests had also had things stolen including money and wallets and our friends car keys which also left them stranded and still no park manager to be seen. Quiet disgusting to be honest. We then had forensic come to the park for finger prints and then finally a lady strolled over, had a brief conversation with two of the guests, then left. I've worked in a holiday village and there is absolutely no way we would have multiple ppl standing around with police after a car was stolen and not have a manager or park representative attend. The guests that had their car stolen on Monday morning and our other friends who had their car keys stolen (our friends) have still not had anyone from the park come and have any kind of conversation with them and its now Tuesday! Absolutely disgusting. It's clear people outside of the park are aware there are no boom gates and target that park. If you plan to stay there, lock your van and cars. Someone's car was stolen and another had their keys stolen which makes them stranded and you haven't even gone and had a conversation or offered a free nights accommodation! It's actually embarrassing that the people in that park are not capable of showing any compassion or empathy for incidents occurring in their accommodation.

This was probably the best park we encountered during our road trip, sadly we only spent one night here as we booked at short notice to avoid free camping during a high fire risk period.

The pitches and facilities here are all top notch, plenty of things to do for everyone, and the location is fantastic. Staff in reception were great.

Had to cancel our powered site booking for September in January and was told there was a $50 cancellation fee even when 8 months clear of the booking.

I complained and was told they would be in touch but never heard back from them.

Park is nice I had been going here for 20 years, big 4 policy leaves a lot to be desired.

We stayed for just one night and, unfortunately, couldn’t take full advantage of all the facilities since we arrived late. However, the holiday park offers plenty of amenities, including a pool, spa, and children’s playground—perfect for those planning a longer stay.
Stayed for over night Small but nice cabin

This place is pure bliss. Close enough to Melbourne for a weekend stay and far enough away to feel like you're in a whole new world. The amazing  customer service is the icing on the cake. I've been here twice in 2 months and am looking to book my next stay very soon. If you want to experience nature with the comfort of air conditioning and a comfortable bed, be sure to book a glamping pod for your stay.
Very nice will visit again
Great family venue. Kids loved the activities and feeding the birds. Lovely venue with lakes and rivers surrounding it
Always love coming to big4 is one is one of our top places to stay 😁
Great for families and those with kids! So much to do here..everyone was very friendly. Staff were really nice and helpful.
Great facilities and excellent sites.

We are new to camping. When we booked the camp site, the staff was very helpful and patient in answering our questions. We camped for 3 days. Facilities were clean and well maintained throughout our stay. There was a kiosk for the much needed cold drinks and coffee to cope the unpredictable weather.

We went for the first time after seeing such great reviews.

The amenities were so dirty. The female showers had tiles pulled up and just laying there, a dirty nappy sat by a toilet all day and the floors were disgusting. The pools were also filthy.
We also were next to a large group who were very loud for both nights, did burn outs on electric scooters in front of our tents, spraying dirt all over us and continually kicked balls into our tent and in our space. They also smoked shisha pipes in big groups too, which wasn’t great for our young kids or our environment. We left a day early after one teenager from this group threw a hard cricket ball at our 8yr olds face. We tried to speak to the manager when leaving a day early and he was busy. I then rang back twice and sent an email before he replied telling us that he won’t refund us for the day we left early for and what did we expect when camping! We expected a bit of common respect and mindfulness with other guests and support from management when groups are disrespectful. Don’t expect any support from the manager as he doesn’t care, just happy to take your money. We will be reporting this to Big4 head office as we have been Big4 members for over 5yrs and have never experienced this kind of treatment from management before. Don’t go, it’s a waste of money. Go to Big4 Bellarine instead, where they look after all guests.

Don't bother with this overpriced, overcrowded gong show. The place has lost a lot of it's charm it once had with location and access to nature. So many cabins have been crammed in that isnt an enjoyable campground anymore. They also charge $123 for an unpowered site which really is just finding a spot on a sloping grassy area where you find your own space to pitch a tent. The campground seems to be all about making money from as many people as possible. Staff are unable to keep up with cleanliness across the park across crucial amenities including bathrooms, toilets and kitchens. The current recreational facilities like the pool is completely unfit for the numbers of guests staying. But I guess pools don't bring in the revenue!
Very clean, wildlife next to the camping spot.

We’d brought our kids here once before in winter and we had a pleasant enough stay. We should have known better than to come in summer, but having stayed at a different big4 location for a week last summer and not having any problems, we thought one night here would be fine. Big mistake. Location, amenities, etc were fine, but the noise late at night was atrocious. Loud music and kids singing and screaming at the top of their lungs while their parents, I assume, were off somewhere drinking by the pond or whatever. We *did* call the after hours line, who rocked up to the noisy group to be warmly greeted as a mate and the noise… continued on. Ended up driving ourselves home at midnight rather than risk an entire sleepless night of frustration.
First time visitor ..very clean with all the facilities to make the stay comfortable close to Shops and attractions..

Stayed on a powered sight and such a lovely experience being close to Badger Creek. Great facilities for kids, jumping pillow, pool etc. Very clean bathroom and kitchen facilities and staff and very friendly, welcoming and helpful which seems rare these days! We will be back.

Best park we have stayed at. Fantastic camp kitchen with 2x TV's,lounges, fridges, BBQ's plenty of table space and chairs. New amenities, just amazing. Caravan sites on cement slab with grass area beside, staff friendly, altogether we would recommend to stay here
Fantastic holiday park. So many activities. Beautifully clean and maintained. Great staff and amenities. Great for families. Highly recommend

Loved the cabin with two bedrooms and bathrooms. Children loved the swimming pool. The birds were amazing, with kookaburras, king parrots and  crimson parrots visiting.  Also close to the Healsvile Sanctuary.
Fantastic campsite and lovely and courteous staff. Highly recommended!

Amazing caravan park! I would suggest the modern newer section for sites but that’s personal preference. The park has everything you can think of plus more. The playgrounds are both great sizes for kids. There is a steep hill between new section and old that isn’t overly kid friendly for biking around but just have to be watching them as it’s a bit of a blind corner for cars too. Staff very friendly and welcoming. The toilets in new section are the most fancy I’ve seen in a caravan park. Soaps provided in showers which is a massive bonus. Please look after this gem if you stay so everyone can enjoy it. So much to see and do in the area! Heated container pool the kids loved!
Family friendly park with lots to do and ample space to enjoy nature and the surroundings. Great amenities and facilities. Definitely would recommend.

This is one of the best camping sites and accommodations around. Close to everything and even food trucks that visit you. Drop the kids off and let them create a new tribe with other kids just like in Lord of the Flies and then take em home at the end of the trip, relaxed.

One minor complaint …. $30 for a bag of firewood. Head to servo or Bunnings and get your own wood. Make the room for it. Maybe it’s the norm here but it’s the most expensive bag of wood I have ever laid in my life. So plan ahead if you need fire!
Well maintained, affordable & absolutely beautiful area to stay. 10/10 would come back again.

As a regular big4 camper this park is outstanding. Has the best of everything.   Nature.  Parks. Activities. Food trucks. A pool.  Open spaces. A stream. Beware the kookaburra that steals the food from your hand.
Healesville big 4 completely exceeded our expectations and the staff and facilities were incredible. Looking forward to visiting again.

The place is beautiful and the facilities were great. Unfortunately that was ruined by a disrespectful people of 150. The kids were constantly unsupervised on  the jumping castle and were having wresting matches. When the staff came to deflate the jumping castle the children were still jumping on it and decided to have a dance party. Unfortunately at this point i had to make the 2 hour drive back home as my little ones couldn't settle with all the noise. There were kids constantly walking through the sites showing no respect and obviously no parents in sight.

Staff obviously don't care they made their money for the weekend so nothing was going to happen. Maybe call up the park to make sure no big groups are staying on the weekend if you choose to book because you are on your own with a massive group of people with a mob mentality.

Honestly I had high hopes, it's a beautiful place with well kept facilities and the staff were friendly, however at 10pm, there is very loud music (not even good music) and shouting. There was a ute doing laps all evening with a dozen people (legit, i counted them) in the back just before, shouting and playing loud music. Sure, have fun, but these people have zero consideration for other paying visitors. People driving around honking their horns and screaming conversations at one another. The holiday park staff don't really seem to police behaviour, so this place is basically a zoo when packed. I am fully expecting a half baked reply from "the owner" saying i should have said something to the after hours line like in other reviews with similar complaints, my pre-emptive reply is sorry champ but i was too busy trying to help my wife settle our little ones, the last thing I am thinking of is ways to tell you to do your job that i paid for you to do. I even took one of our kids for a walk to the playground which was full of rubbish, while I was gone some other kids were trying to pinch our fire wood while they thought my wife wasn't looking! Struth. Don't come here if you want a quiet weekend with young kids. Do come here if you're a loud bogan. 🤷‍♂️ probably won't be back based on this, it just all seems pretty shabby, unsafe and unreasonable, especially for what you pay here - sadly I've spoken to a few others who are just as shocked. Shame, really.
Rippa place lovely staff. Can't fault
it was the best time i've ever had and I definitely recomend this place to EVERYONE.
最接近大自然的公園，地方好大，設施齊全，房間乾淨。小孩特別喜歡踩單車的地方。一早起身在房間門口就可以餵鸚鵡，是一次好特別的體驗，不會忘記。孩子好喜歡這公園。推薦有小朋友嘅家庭。

The log pod accommodation at BIG4 in Yarra Valley offers a unique and cozy retreat amidst nature. Its charming, compact design provides an intimate and serene environment, perfect for those seeking a peaceful getaway. The structure, resembling a cabin or pod, blends seamlessly into the surrounding bushland, offering an authentic nature experience while still providing comfort.

Despite its small size, the cabin's wooden aesthetic offers warmth and a rustic feel, giving guests a sense of being close to nature without sacrificing modern amenities. The surrounding trees enhance the atmosphere, creating a calm and picturesque backdrop. Ideal for couples or solo travelers, this accommodation is perfect for unwinding after a day of exploring the Yarra Valley’s wineries, hiking trails, or other outdoor activities.

Furthermore, there are many other amenities including bbq and kitchen area, swimming pool, playground for kids etc. It is definitely the best choice for family with kids.

Overall, the log pod at BIG4 in Yarra Valley is a delightful and comfortable choice for a nature escape, offering both charm and convenience in one of Australia’s most scenic regions. Highly recommended!!!!

2021.12
